cost: $0 (GPU local + reuse of byte-verified offline measurements; Gate-2 reuse precedent)
method: Embedder ceiling is a device-invariant model-weights property; consolidate the FULL s15a probe (eu7 re-clear + eu8 + hard subset + paired-bootstrap CI + cpu cost) and the eu-0 raw-recall sweep, and confirm device-invariance on the GPU.

gpu_confirmation.conclusion: GPU and CPU vectors are f32-equivalent -> the ceiling is device-invariant; the reused CPU measurement holds on GPU.

verdict.verdict: KEEP bge-small. No candidate clears the swap gate net of re-whiten/eu7 re-clear + cost. A productized swap is out of 0.8.11 (HITL #2).

per_candidate_block_reason.bge-base: projected_eu7 0.7855 < 0.9 (fails 1-bit eu7 re-clear); hard-subset margin CI-lo -0.0036 <= 0 (no clearance)
per_candidate_block_reason.e5-base-v2: projected_eu7 0.896 < 0.9 (fails 1-bit eu7 re-clear); hard-subset margin CI-lo -0.0061 <= 0 (no clearance)
per_candidate_block_reason.gte-base: measurement FAILED (IndexError: index 126396013669456 is out of bounds); not in-library feasible
per_candidate_block_reason.nomic: hard-subset margin CI-lo -0.0085 <= 0 (no clearance); not cpu_feasible (36.1ms/q > 3x base)

verdict.eu0_reconciliation: eu-0 raw recall@10 (fanout K=256): bge-small=0.933, bge-base=0.964, e5-small-v2=0.664. EXP-M4 CONFIRMS the eu-0 ordering (bge-base highest raw recall, e5 worst) but REVISES the naive 'bigger is better' conclusion: net of the 1-bit eu7 re-clear (bge-base projected_eu7=0.7855 < 0.90), the hard-subset margin, and 2x cost, bge-base does NOT clear the swap gate -> keep bge-small.
verdict.keep_unless: Keep bge-small UNLESS a candidate simultaneously (a) clears the 0.90 projected_eu7 floor after re-whiten, (b) shows a hard-subset margin CI-lo > 0 vs bge-small, and (c) is cpu_feasible (<= 3x base latency) or HITL accepts the GPU/cost tradeoff.
